tex:
Helga buys some items to do some knitting.
\newline

(a) Complete Helga's bill from one shop.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Item & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
2 pairs of knitting needles at \$4.95 a pair & \\
\hline
6 buttons at \$0.65 each & \\
\hline
1 knitting pattern at \$3.60 & 3.60 \\
\hline
 Total & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) Helga also buys 8 balls of wool from another shop.
\newline

Each ball costs \$3.12.
\newline

Helga pays with a \$50 note.
\newline

Work out the amount of change she receives.
\newline

(c) Helga knits some squares.
\newline

Each square is either white, pink or blue.
\newline

The number of squares are in the ratio white : pink : blue $= 5 : 3 : 2$.
\newline

30 squares are blue.
\newline

Show that Helga knits 150 squares.
\newline

(d) Helga uses some of the squares to make a rectangular blanket.
\newline

The blanket is 6 squares long and 4 squares wide.
\newline

(i)  Calculate the percentage of the 150 squares she uses to make this blanket.
\newline

 Each square has side length $15\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Work out the perimeter of this blanket.
\newline

Give your answer in metres.
\newline

_rawBody:
Helga buys some items to do some knitting.

\begin{parts}
\item Complete Helga's bill from one shop.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|}
\hline
Item & Cost (\$) \\
\hline
2 pairs of knitting needles at \$4.95 a pair & \\
\hline
6 buttons at \$0.65 each & \\
\hline
1 knitting pattern at \$3.60 & 3.60 \\
\hline
\hfill Total & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
\Marks{3}

\item Helga also buys 8 balls of wool from another shop.

Each ball costs \$3.12\,.

Helga pays with a \$50 note.

Work out the amount of change she receives.

\AnswerLine[\$][]
\Marks{2}

\item Helga knits some squares.

Each square is either white, pink or blue.

The number of squares are in the ratio white : pink : blue $= 5 : 3 : 2$.

30 squares are blue.

Show that Helga knits 150 squares.
\Marks{2}

\item Helga uses some of the squares to make a rectangular blanket.

The blanket is 6 squares long and 4 squares wide.

\begin{subparts}
\subpart Calculate the percentage of the 150 squares she uses to make this blanket.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{2}

\subpart Each square has side length 15\,cm.

Work out the perimeter of this blanket.

Give your answer in metres.

\AnswerLine[][m]
\Marks{3}

\end{subparts}

\end{parts}

tex:
Triangles $A$, $B$ and $C$ are shown on the grid.
\newline

(a) 
Describe fully the **single** transformation that maps

(i)  triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

 triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline

On the grid,

(i)  reflect triangle $A$ in the line $y = 0$,

 translate triangle $A$ by the vector $\displaystyle\binom{-7}{1}$.
\newline

content.content: Describe fully the \textbf{single} transformation that maps
triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.
\newline
On the grid,
reflect triangle $A$ in the line $y = 0$,

translate triangle $A$ by the vector $\displaystyle\binom{-7}{1}$.
\newline

tex:
Miguel works in an office.
\newline

(a) 
It takes Miguel 40 minutes to drive to work.
\newline

(i)  He leaves home at 07$\,$45.
\newline

What time does he arrive at work?

 Miguel drives to work at an average speed of $57\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

Show that he drives $38\,\text{km}$.
\newline

White paper costs $w$ cents per sheet and pink paper costs $p$ cents per sheet.
\newline

Miguel uses 56 sheets of white paper and 21 sheets of pink paper.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $w$ and $p$, for the total cost, in cents, of the paper he uses.
\newline

Miguel has a closed box of pens.
\newline

The box is in the shape of a cuboid measuring $20\,\text{cm}$ by $12\,\text{cm}$ by $7\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the surface area of the box.
\newline

content.content:
It takes Miguel 40 minutes to drive to work.
\newline

He leaves home at 07$\,$45.
\newline

Miguel drives to work at an average speed of $57\,\text{km/h}$.
\newline

\newline

White paper costs $w$ cents per sheet and pink paper costs $p$ cents per sheet.
\newline

Miguel uses 56 sheets of white paper and 21 sheets of pink paper.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $w$ and $p$, for the total cost, in cents, of the paper he uses.
\newline

Miguel has a closed box of pens.
\newline

The box is in the shape of a cuboid measuring $20\,\text{cm}$ by $12\,\text{cm}$ by $7\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the surface area of the box.
\newline

tex:
Miguel records the length of time of each telephone call he receives, correct to the nearest minute.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{cccccccccc}
7 & 15 & 6 & 28 & 8 & 21 & 17 & 19 & 20 & 12 \\
11 & 19 & 12 & 3 & 20 & 23 & 14 & 9 & 4 & 18 \\
\end{tabular}
(a) 
Complete the frequency table.
\newline

You may use the tally column to help you.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time (minutes) & Tally & Frequency \\
\hline
$0 - 5$ & & \\
\hline
$6 - 10$ & & \\
\hline
$11 - 15$ & & \\
\hline
$16 - 20$ & & \\
\hline
$21 - 25$ & & \\
\hline
$26 - 30$ & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Draw a bar chart to show this information.
\newline

Complete the scale on the frequency axis.
\newline

Use the bar chart to write down the modal group.
\newline

qtype: identifying number types, fraction of quantity, powers and roots, basic operations
tex:
(a) 
Find

(i)  a multiple of 3 between 70 and 80,

 a factor of 63 between 5 and 10,

 a cube number between 60 and 90,

 the reciprocal of 7.
\newline

Work out $\displaystyle\frac{2}{7}$ of 84.
\newline

Find the value of

(i)  $\sqrt[3]{3375}$,

 $12^0$.
\newline

Rana hires a car.
\newline

The cost is \$74 per day plus a delivery cost of \$17.50.
\newline

Rana pays a total of \$461.50.
\newline

Calculate the number of days that Rana hires the car.
\newline

A train to town $A$ leaves a station every 25 minutes.
\newline

A train to town $B$ leaves the same station every 45 minutes.
\newline

Both trains leave at 08$\,$00.
\newline

Find the next time both trains leave together.
\newline

qtype: simplifying expressions, substitution, factorisation, solving linear equations, indices laws, forming and solving equations
tex:
(a) 
Simplify.
\newline
\[5g - 3h - 7g + 6h\]

$j = 4k + 7m$

Find the value of $j$ when $k = -5$ and $m = 6$.
\newline

Factorise completely.
\newline
\[14x^3 + 49x\]

Solve.
\newline
\[8(3t - 9) = 108\]

t
(i)  $9^{24} \div 9^w = 9^5$

Find the value of $w$.
\newline

 $4x^2 = 256$

Find the value of $x$.
\newline

Ranjit's age is $x$ years.
\newline

Suzi's age is 3 times Ranjit's age.
\newline

Juan's age is 4 years more than Suzi's age.
\newline

The total of their ages is 46 years.
\newline

Use this information to write down an equation and solve it to find the value of $x$.
\newline
